behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about extraction quality from an AI reviewer, not just deterministic parsing. Real failures come from model interpretation/recall (and possible run-to-run variation), which mocks or pure unit tests cannot expose.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model against a labeled contract set containing explicit and implicit key terms; assert required recall per term type and verify no term class is systematically missed across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: Detecting indirect/euphemistic risk language is semantic judgment quality from the model. Deterministic mocks can pass while the real model misses subtle phrasing.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real risk-assessor model with contracts that include policy-violating euphemistic language and policy-compliant near-misses; assert target precision/recall against human labels under the configured risk policy.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Correctness here depends on model-based regulatory interpretation for a jurisdiction, not only wiring. A mock would hide real misclassification of compliance status.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real compliance-checker model on jurisdiction-specific gold contracts (compliant and non-compliant), assert decision accuracy and required explanation linkage to relevant requirements for that jurisdiction.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (e.g., extractor says "standard" but risk assessor says "high risk"), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff/contract between reviewer outputs and merge logic (conflict resolution plus attribution). A pure unit test of isolated logic may miss real schema/adapter mismatches between modules.
behaviors.test_description: Execute reviewer-output adapters plus merge module together using realistic reviewer payloads that intentionally conflict on the same clause; assert merged report retains both assessments and correct reviewer attribution fields.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging into a unified report — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any individual reviewer's findings.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires end-to-end orchestration across multiple real agentic steps (three AI reviewers + merge). Real failures include async fan-out/fan-in loss, partial result drops, and cross-step interactions only visible in full pipeline execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run the production-like pipeline on real contract inputs with all three real reviewer calls enabled in parallel, then verify completion, per-reviewer result presence, and that merged output contains every finding with source attribution.
